title: Floral, Fluffy Kenyan Potato Buns Bao
description: Light, airy Kenyan potato buns served in a ttukbaegi earthenware cook vessel over a hot stone plate, perfect for a raw-friendly meal.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, potato, and yeast, then gradually add warm water to form a dough.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a warm, draft-free place to rise for 2 hours.
After the dough has risen, divide it into 6 equal portions and shape each into a bun.
Place the buns in a ttukbaegi earthenware cook vessel over a hot stone plate to cook for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
While the buns are cooking, mix together the melted butter and chopped herbs in a separate bowl.
Once the buns are cooked, brush them with the herb butter mixture and serve warm.
Serve the buns in a hot stone plate to keep them warm and fresh.
Offer a side of raw vegetables, such as carrots and cucumbers, to complement the dish.
For an added touch, garnish with edible flowers or microgreens.
To make the dish more substantial, consider serving with a side of protein-rich accompaniments, such as grilled chicken or avocado.
Consider making the dish in advance and refrigerating or freezing the buns for a quick snack or meal later in the day.
When serving the buns, be sure to serve them in a hot stone plate to keep them warm and fresh.
